---
jupyter: python3
format: 
  pdf:
    echo: false
    geometry:
      - top=5mm        
      - left=5mm
      - right=5mm
      - bottom=20mm
      - headheight=35mm 
      - headsep=5mm 
      - includehead=true 
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          \usepackage{tabularx}
          \usepackage{multirow}
          \usepackage{graphicx}
          \usepackage{xcolor}
          \usepackage{fancyhdr}
          \usepackage{lastpage}
          \usepackage{multicol}
          \usepackage{ifthen}

          \pagestyle{fancy}
          \fancyhf{} 
          \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
          \newcounter{score}
          
          \fancyhead[C]{
            \noindent
            \bgroup
            \def\arraystretch{1.4}
            \begin{tabularx}{\textwidth}{|p{5.5cm}|p{1.8cm}|X|p{1.2cm}|p{1.8cm}|}
            \hline
            \multirow{5}{5.5cm}{
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \includegraphics[width=4.5cm]{logo} \\
                \fontsize{6pt}{7pt}\selectfont
                \textbf{CIVIL \& ENVIRONMENTAL ENGINEERING,}\\
                SKEMPTON BUILDING, IMPERIAL COLLEGE\\
                LONDON, SOUTH KENSINGTON CAMPUS,\\
                LONDON SW7 2AZ.\\[3mm]
                \normalsize\textbf{CALCULATION SHEET}
            }
            & \textbf{Job No.} & & \textbf{Sheet} & \textbf{Ref} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \textbf{Job Title} & Steel Design Coursework & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\thepage\ of \pageref{LastPage}} \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multicolumn{4}{p{\dimexpr\textwidth-5.5cm-8\tabcolsep-5\arrayrulewidth\relax}|}{
                \textbf{Subject} \newline
                \vspace*{1mm}
                \centerline{ Scheme B Truss Member Calculation }
                \vspace*{1mm}
            } \\ \cline{2-5}
            & \multirow{2}{*}{\textbf{Client}} & \textbf{Made by} \quad  Pablo Peng & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date} \quad 03/03/2026} \\ \cline{3-5}
            & & \textbf{Checked by} \quad  Pablo Peng & \multicolumn{2}{l|}{\textbf{Date}} \\ \hline
            \end{tabularx}
            \egroup
          }
---
\Large 
\linespread{1.4}
\selectfont

```{python}
weet = [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6, 1.7, 1.8, 1.9, 1.10]
weft = [-2465, 6362, -4318, -2655, -2468, -1168, 2160, -3.16, -4034, 2160]
weeb = [2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9, 2.10]
weefb = [-2073, 5349, -3631, -2232, -2075, -482, 1816, -3.04, -3391, 1914]
wee = weet + weeb
wef = weft + weefb
wef1t, wef2t = 2656, 2656
wef1b, wef2b = 2233, 2233
wert, werb = 2061, 1733
wed = 49.98

nset = [1.1, 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5, 1.6, 1.7, 1.8]
nsft = [-2655, 11.82, 3284, -1986, -3449, 2001, 4345, -4612]
nseb = [2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8]
nsfb = [-2901, 13.16, 3588, -2170, -4385, 2188, 5517, -5503]
nse = nset + nseb
nsf = nsft + nsfb
nsf1t, nsf2t = 606, 824
nsf1b, nsf2b = 1181, 1521
nsd = 7.75
```
#
#
#
#
#
#
#
#
#
#
# Loads and deflection at the west side of the truss
\begin{description}
    \item[]The column loads at 6th floor, $F_{1,1}$ = $F_{1,2}$ = `{python} wef1t` kN
    \item[]The column loads at 3rd floor, $F_{2,1}$ = $F_{2,2}$ = `{python} wef1b` kN
    \item[]Maximum deflection along the span = `{python} wed` mm < 50 mm
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
#
#
#
#
#

# Axial forces in west side truss
```{python}
#| output: asis

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Wrap the table after a fixed number of elements so it fits the page width.
max_elements_per_row = 6
rows = []

for start in range(0, len(wee), max_elements_per_row):
  element_chunk = wee[start:start + max_elements_per_row]
  force_chunk = wef[start:start + max_elements_per_row]
  pad_count = max_elements_per_row - len(element_chunk)

  rows.append(['Element'] + [f"{float(e)}" for e in element_chunk] + [''] * pad_count)
  rows.append(['Axial force (kN)'] + [f"{f:.2f}" for f in force_chunk] + [''] * pad_count)

col = ['Description'] + [f"Value {index}" for index in range(1, max_elements_per_row + 1)]
table_horizontal = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=col)

latex_lines = [
  r"\begin{tabular}{|l|" + "c|" * max_elements_per_row + "}",
  r"\hline",
  " & ".join(col) + r" \\",
  r"\hline"
]

for row_index, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
  latex_lines.append(" & ".join(row) + r" \\")
  if row_index % 2 == 0:
    latex_lines.append(r"\hline")

latex_lines.append(r"\end{tabular}")
latex_table = "\n".join(latex_lines)

print(r"\normalfont")
print(r"\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.2}")
print(r"\setlength{\tabcolsep}{11pt}")
print(latex_table)
```
Note: Positive values indicate tension, negative values indicate compression.

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
#
#
#
#
#
# Loads and deflection at the north side of the truss
\begin{description}
    \item[]The corner column loads at 8th floor, $F_{1,1,a}$ = `{python} nsf1t` kN
    \item[]The reaction force from west side truss at 8th floor, $F_{1,1,b}$ = `{python} wert` kN
    \item[]$F_{1,1} = F_{1,1,a} + F_{1,1,b}$ = `{python} nsf1t + wert` kN
    \item[]The perimeter column loads at 8th floor, $F_{1,2}$ = `{python} nsf2t` kN
    \item[]The corner column loads at 5th floor, $F_{2,1,a}$ = `{python} nsf1b` kN
    \item[]The reaction force from west side truss at 5th floor, $F_{2,1,b}$ = `{python} werb` kN
    \item[]$F_{2,1} = F_{2,1,a} + F_{2,1,b}$ = `{python} nsf1b + werb` kN
    \item[]The perimeter column loads at 5th floor, $F_{2,2}$ = `{python} nsf2b` kN
    \item[]Maximum deflection = `{python} nsd` mm < 50 mm
\end{description}

{{< pagebreak >}}

#
#
#
#
#
#
#

# Axial forces in north side truss
```{python}
#| output: asis

# Wrap the table after a fixed number of elements so it fits the page width.
max_elements_per_row = 6
rows = []
for start in range(0, len(nse), max_elements_per_row):
  element_chunk = nse[start:start + max_elements_per_row]
  force_chunk = nsf[start:start + max_elements_per_row]
  pad_count = max_elements_per_row - len(element_chunk)

  rows.append(['Element'] + [f"{float(e)}" for e in element_chunk] + [''] * pad_count)
  rows.append(['Axial force (kN)'] + [f"{f:.2f}" for f in force_chunk] + [''] * pad_count)
col1 = ['Description'] + [f"Value {index}" for index in range(1, max_elements_per_row + 1)]
table_horizontal = pd.DataFrame(rows, columns=col1)
latex_lines = [
  r"\begin{tabular}{|l|" + "c|" * max_elements_per_row + "}",
  r"\hline",
  " & ".join(col1) + r" \\",
  r"\hline"
]
for row_index, row in enumerate(rows, start=1):
  latex_lines.append(" & ".join(row) + r" \\")
  if row_index % 2 == 0:
    latex_lines.append(r"\hline")
latex_lines.append(r"\end{tabular}")
latex_table = "\n".join(latex_lines)    
print(r"\normalfont")
print(r"\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.2}")
print(r"\setlength{\tabcolsep}{11pt}")
print(latex_table)
```
Note: Positive values indicate tension, negative values indicate compression.
